title: Vema Hydrogen to Deliver Clean Hydrogen for California Data Centers to Meet Surging Energy Demand
content: Vema Hydrogen, developer of disruptive Engineered Mineral Hydrogen (EMH), has entered a Hydrogen Purchase and Sale Agreement with Verne, a provider of on-site power and cooling solutions for data centers. Through this 10-year agreement, Verne will leverage Vemaâ€™s clean hydrogen to provide affordable, reliable, and low-emission power for data center customers across California, with operations beginning as soon as 2028. Vema plans to increase production to over 36,000 metric tons per year of EMH to meet the surging energy demand driven by artificial intelligence. Vemaâ€™s proprietary technology uses geoscience to create high-purity, sustainable hydrogen from naturally occurring reactions below the earth's surface. Verne CEO Ted McKlveen emphasized the industry's need for scalable solutions like EMH. Vema CEO Pierre Levin noted that this partnership ensures access to affordable, clean power that is not dependent on state or federal incentives, positioning the company to support AI-driven power hubs globally.
author: Analysts Account
